fed_stance.label: Neutral hold
fed_stance.source: CME FedWatch for 30 Apr 2026 showed approximately 0.0% ease, 99.5% no change, and 0.5% hike, so the Fed stance remains a neutral hold.

real_yields.label: Flat
real_yields.source: FRED DFII10 showed 1.95 on 2026-04-10, and the latest official move remains too small to qualify as a directional real-yield shift under the methodology.

usd_dxy.label: Weak/falling
usd_dxy.source: TradingView DXY was 98.079, down 0.30% on the day and down 0.99% over five days, which keeps the dollar factor in weak/falling territory.

risk_mood.label: Balanced
risk_mood.source: Cboe VIX spot was 18.31, which remains inside the methodology's balanced 15-20 band rather than a high-stress regime.

vix_direction.label: Falling
vix_direction.source: The VIX was down 4.24% (-0.81) from the prior close, so volatility direction is classified as falling.

growth_narrative.label: Slowing
growth_narrative.source: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remained at 1.3% for 2026:Q1 after having been higher in the prior repository baseline, so the U.S. growth narrative remains slowing.

oil_supply_shock.label: Tightening
oil_supply_shock.source: The EIA's recent outlook still treats the Hormuz closure and related outages as key oil-market drivers, so the supply-shock classification remains tightening.

oil_inventories.label: Building
oil_inventories.source: The latest EIA weekly petroleum summary for the week ended 2026-04-03 reported a 3.1 million barrel rise in U.S. commercial crude inventories, which is a bearish inventory build.

gold_etf_flows.label: Outflowing
gold_etf_flows.source: The World Gold Council reported record March gold-ETF outflows totaling about US$12 billion, so the ETF-flow signal remains negative.

credit_spreads.label: Widening
credit_spreads.source: FRED high-yield OAS moved from 2.90 to 2.94 on the latest official print, which classifies as widening credit spreads in this run.

sox.label: Rising
sox.source: TradingView showed SOX at 9,039.52, up 1.68% on the day and 6.37% over five days, preserving a rising semiconductor signal.

move_index.label: Rising
move_index.source: TradingView showed MOVE at 74.4185, up 3.14% on the day, so the rates-volatility factor is rising and therefore negative for Nasdaq under the methodology.

yield_curve_2s10s.label: Steepening
yield_curve_2s10s.source: FRED T10Y2Y increased to 0.52 on 2026-04-13 from 0.50 on 2026-04-10, which classifies the curve as steepening.

copper.label: Unverified / flat
copper.source: Live copper pages remained incomplete in this session, so the copper factor is conservatively scored flat rather than guessed.

ecb_stance.label: Mildly hawkish hold
ecb_stance.source: The ECB page was not cleanly extractable in-browser, but the latest available official ECB communication and repository backdrop still support a mildly hawkish hold classification rather than an easing signal.

rate_diff_eur_usd.label: Stable
rate_diff_eur_usd.source: With the Fed neutral and the ECB on hold rather than actively easing or hiking in today's verified set, the near-term EUR-USD policy differential is best treated as stable.

eurozone_growth.label: Slowing / downgraded
eurozone_growth.source: The latest repository-verified ECB growth backdrop still reflects a downgraded euro-area outlook, so eurozone growth remains classified as slowing.

rba_stance.label: Hawkish/tightening
rba_stance.source: The RBA's 17 March 2026 decision raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10% and highlighted upside inflation risks, so the RBA stance remains hawkish.

rate_diff_aud_usd.label: Widening vs USD
rate_diff_aud_usd.source: A hawkish 4.10% RBA backdrop against a neutral Fed still leaves the AUD-USD short-rate differential supportive for AUD.

risk_sentiment_aud.label: Neutral
risk_sentiment_aud.source: The broad volatility regime is balanced rather than decisively risk-on or risk-off, so the AUD-specific sentiment factor remains neutral.

china_growth.label: Improving but slowing
china_growth.source: China manufacturing PMI was 50.8 in March 2026, down from 52.1 in February but still above 50, which supports a conservative positive classification.

boj_stance.label: Neutral hold
boj_stance.source: The 19 March 2026 BoJ statement kept the uncollateralized overnight call rate at around 0.75% and described financial conditions as accommodative, so the safer classification for this run is neutral hold.

rate_diff_jpy_usd.label: Improving for JPY
rate_diff_jpy_usd.source: A neutral Fed combined with the post-normalization BoJ backdrop still keeps the policy-divergence story favorable for yen futures.

geopolitical_risk.label: Elevated
geopolitical_risk.source: The EIA continues to frame Hormuz-related disruption as a live oil-market driver, so geopolitical risk for crude remains elevated.
